mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate signals a robust labor market, which underpins strong consumer confidence and spending, thereby supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The S&P 500's 20-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with the inverse of the unemployment rate over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the federal funds rate indicates a tightening monetary policy, which raises the cost of capital for businesses and consumers, leading to a slowdown in economic activity and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25 percentage points over three months, the S&P 500's 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Robust growth in retail sales combined with consistent payroll additions signifies strong consumer demand and a healthy employment environment. This dual strength drives corporate revenue and profit growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction effect between the month-over-month growth rates of RSAFS and PAYEMS does not exhibit a statistically significant positive relationship with 20-day S&P 500 forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts above a critical threshold during a post-recession recovery period indicates renewed consumer and business confidence, stimulating construction and related sectors, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: During identified post-recession recovery periods, housing starts exceeding 800,000 units do not lead to significantly positive 60-day S&P 500 returns, as measured by a t-test p-value > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects increasing manufacturing output and overall business activity, indicating a healthy supply side of the economy that directly supports corporate revenues and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short-term horizon.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward returns of the S&P 500 do not exhibit a positive correlation with the month-over-month change in INDPRO over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid and significant rise in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and potential economic contraction, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over six months, the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns are not statistically significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of persistently high inflation and sluggish retail sales growth indicates a stagflationary environment where consumer purchasing power is eroded and demand weakens, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate margins are squeezed over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annual growth > 3% AND RSAFS annual growth < 2%
failure_condition: In periods where annual CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% and RSAFS growth is below 2%, the S&P 500's 60-day forward returns are predominantly positive or flat, with no significant negative trend.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of sustained economic expansion, real GDP growth exceeding 2% annually indicates robust overall economic health, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: In identified periods of sustained economic expansion where GDPC1 annual growth is above 2%, the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive trend (p-value > 0.05).
